# **Users Overview**

Users are company members and other individuals who can work in evalink talos under the Company account.

evalink talos operates the following user-related terms:

* User Roles  
* User Types  
* User Profiles

Only Administrator can invite and manage users, create new User Roles and User Types.

## **User Roles**

Each user has a *role* that defines their level of permissions. There are four preset User Roles in evalink talos. They cannot be changed or deleted.

Administrators can also create custom User Roles based on the preset User Roles. A custom User Role allows you to configure a custom set of permissions.

You can view all the User Roles for your company under **Company** \> **Settings** \> **Users**.

The following preset User Roles exist in evalink talos:

* Administrator – has a full set of permissions  
  There can be several Administrators under one Company.  
* Manager – this User Role is intended for managing the work of users with Operator and Operator Minimal User Roles. Like Administrator, Manager can create and manage major evalink talos objects: workflows, sites and site groups, schedules, etc., but access to some features is restricted  
  For example, Manager cannot invite users and edit global alarm settings.  
* Operator – this User Role is intended for alarm processing. Operator has access to all evalink talos interface pages, but, for the most part, cannot create or manage objects, only has permissions for viewing  
* Operator Minimal – this User Role is also intended for alarm processing. As compared to Operator, Operator Minimal has access to a limited number of interface pages

**info**

Both Operator and Operator Minimal can create and edit certain evalink talos objects, if required to do so by a workflow – for example, if the *Add Schedule Entry* step is included in a manual workflow.

For details on how to create user User Roles, see section [Work with User Settings \> Create a New User Role](https://documentation.evalink.io/talos/admin/work-with-users/manage-user-settings#create-a-new-user-type).

## **User Types**

User Types allow to fine-tune user experience by combining User Roles with certain pre-configured work environments such as Test Types and Workspaces, and access permissions such as selected access to Site Groups in your company.

You can view the list of available Test Types on the *Company* \> *Settings* \> *Sites* page. You can view the list of available Workspaces on the *Company* \> *Settings* \> *Alarms* page. Only Administrators can add and modify Test Types and Workspaces.

User Types narrow down the flow of incoming alarms for each user in order to more effectively distribute alarms between available operators.

There are four preset User Types in evalink talos. They cannot be deleted, but Administrators can change their settings. Administrators can also add and delete custom User Types.

The following preset User Types exist in evalink talos:

* Administrator  
  The default User Role for this User Type is *Administrator*. The default Workspace is *Default*. The default Test Type is *System*.  
* Manager  
  The default User Role for this User Type is *Manager*. The default Workspace is *Default*. The default Test Type is *System*.  
* Operator  
  The default User Role for this User Type is *Operator*. The default Workspace is *Default*. The default Test Type is *System*.  
* Operator Minimal  
  The default User Role for this User Type is *Operator Minimal*. The default Workspace is *Default*. The default Test Type is *System*.

By default, the name of preset User Types correspond with the names of preset User Roles. However, there is a difference between User Roles and User Types.

User Role is a kernel setting that defines available user permissions. User Type is the main user-defining quality. It combines a User Role and available working environments (Workspaces and Test Types). When Administrators invite new users, they select a User Type for them when issuing an invite. User Type is also the setting that can be changed for individual users by Administrators later on.

For details on how to create User Types, see section [Work with User Settings \> Create a New User Role](https://documentation.evalink.io/talos/admin/work-with-users/manage-user-settings#create-a-new-user-role).

## **User Profiles**

A user profile is the evalink talos user interface page where you can view all your user information and manage your user activity.

To access your user profile, click the user profile icon account\_circle in the upper right corner and select person **Profile** from the dropdown menu.

For details on managing your user profile, see section [Manage User Profile](https://documentation.evalink.io/talos/admin/work-with-users/manage-user-profile).

## **How Users Are Created**

To create a new user, Administrator sends an invite to the user. The only prerequisite that is required is a valid email address. A user then logs in to evalink talos following the instructions in the invitation and verification emails.

After the first user login, the record of the user appears in the list on the *Company* \> *Users* page. The operations available on this page are described in section [View Users](https://documentation.evalink.io/talos/admin/work-with-users/view-users).

When inviting a user, Administrator selects a User Type from the list of available types which includes preset and custom User Types, see section [Manage User Invites \> Invite a User](https://documentation.evalink.io/talos/admin/work-with-users/manage-user-invites#invite-a-user) for details.

**tip**

After a user accepts the invite and logs into the company and their record appears in the list of company users, administrators can change their User Type. For details, see section [Manage Users \> Edit a User](https://documentation.evalink.io/talos/admin/work-with-users/manage-users#edit-a-user).

## **User-Related Activities**

**![][image1]**

# **Manage User Profile**

## **Viewing User Profile**

***User Profile*** **is a page on the evalink talos user interface where all user information is stored in one place. Any evalink talos user can access their user profile, regardless of the user type or user role.**

**You can access your user profile by clicking on the user profile icon in the upper right corner and selecting Profile from the dropdown menu.**

**The figure below shows the *User Profile* page:**

**![][image2]**

**The following tabs are available on the *User Profile* page:**

* **Profile**  
  **In this tab, you can view all your user details, monitor and manage your user activity and sessions, perform basic user actions.**  
* **Company Management**  
  **In this tab, you can view which companies you are a member of, accept invites and switch between companies without logging out and back in.**

**All the procedures, described below, can be performed on the tabs of the User Profile.**

### **Change User Name**

**In the *User Profile* page, you can change your user name. Your user name is not used to log into evalink talos. It is the name that is associated with your user account. You can see it in evalink talos menu in the bottom left corner and in the list of evalink talos operators on *Alarms* page.**

**To change your user name:**

1. **In the *Profile* tab of the *User Profile* page, scroll to the *Profile Actions* and select Change Name**  
   **A name change dialog opens:**  
   1. **![][image3]**  
2. **Type your First Name and your Last Name in the corresponding fields**  
   **Both fields are obligatory. You have to enter at least 3 characters for a valid First Name and Last Name.**  
3. **Click Let's Start**  
   **In order for the username changes to take place, evalink talos logs you out. You have to log back in to continue work.**  
     
     
     
 


### **Change User Password**

**To change your password:**

1. **In the *Profile* tab of the *User Profile* page, scroll to the *Profile Actions* and select Change Password**  
   **evalink talos sends an email to the email you use to log in.**  
2. **Follow the link in the email you receive from evalink talos**  
   **The password change dialog opens:**  
   **![][image4]**  
3. **Enter your new password into the two fields so that both passwords match**  
   **Your password needs to be at least 8 characters long. You have to use lower and upper case letters, numbers, and special symbols for a valid password.**  
4. **Click \> to confirm your changes**  
   **evalink talos will log you out for the password change to take place. You have to log back in using your new password to continue work.**

### **Viewing Your Sessions**

**You can log into evalink talos using the same username simultaneously from several devices, locations and / or web browsers on the same device.**

**Each of the login instances is considered a session. All current sessions are displayed in your user profile with unique properties.**

**Each current session record has the following properties:**

* **IP address**  
  **The IP address from which you log in. Your current session is marked \[You\] next to the address.**  
* **Last Access**  
  **The time when this session has last been active.**  
* **Location**  
  **The approximate location of the login session. It is narrowed down to a country and a city or town.**  
* **Browser**  
  **The operation system and name and version of the web browser used to log in to evalink talos. For example, Chrome 0 on Win 10\.**  
* **Fingerprint**  
  **A unique stamp marking each individual session.**

**info**

**When you log into evalink talos and there is an active session, evalink talos always notifies you about it upon login.**

### **Invalidate Sessions**

**You can simultaneously log out of talos from all current sessions on all devices using the Invalidate Sessions option without having to manually log out from each session.**

**In most cases, this action is performed for security purposes.**

**To invalidate all sessions, do one of the following:**

* **Click the Invalidate Sessions icon in the bottom-right of the page**  
* **Use the Invalidate Sessions option in the *If your Account Has Been Compromised* area:**

**![][image5]**

**evalink talos terminates all existing sessions on all devices and browsers. You will need to log back in to continue work.**

### **Leave Company**

**To leave a company you are a member of:**

1. **Click the Leave the Company icon in the bottom-right of the page**  
2. **Confirm that you want to leave the company by clicking yes**

**evalink talos displays a message that you are no longer a member of the current company.**

**info**

**When you belong to several companies, leaving one of them will delete your user record from this particular company only. You will remain a user of your other companies.**

**After you have successfully left a company, an Administrator can invite you back in.**

**If you have left a company and are a member of at least one other company, evalink talos will prompt you to log out for the changes to take place. You can afterwards log back in. The company you have left will not be available for you any longer.**

### **Actions in Case your Account Is Compromised**

**If you see any suspicious login sessions in your session list or suspect that someone may have unauthorized access to any of your current sessions, take the following steps as suggested on your *User Profile* page:**

1. **Change your password**  
   **For details, see the procedure above: [Change User Password](https://documentation.evalink.io/talos/admin/work-with-users/manage-user-profile#change-user-password).**  
2. **Invalidate all sessions**  
   **For details, see the procedure above: [Invalidate Sessions](https://documentation.evalink.io/talos/admin/work-with-users/manage-user-profile#invalidate-sessions).**

## **Viewing Company Management**

**The *Company Management* tab contains information about all companies you belong to as a user and about your invites that you haven't accepted yet.**

**In this tab, you can also perform actions, such as switching between your companies, accept invites and create new companies.**

**The figure below shows a pending invite that you haven't accepted yet:**

**![][image6]**

**To accept a pending invite, scroll to the invite record and click Join.**

**The figure below shows a list of companies a user is a member of. An active company is marked with *You are in this company now*.**

**![][image7]**

**To switch from your active company to another one, find the desired company in the list and click \> next to its record.**

### **Join Another Company**

**As an active evalink talos user, you can join another company, provided that a user with Administrator rights for this company has invited you.**

**info**

**Make sure you are logged out of evalink talos before joining a new company through an email invitation.**

**To join a new company:**

1. **Follow the link in your invite email**  
   **You will be taken to evalink talos registration and login dialog.**  
2. **In the registration and login dialog, switch to the *Log in* tab**  
3. **Enter your user credentials and click Log in**  
   **You will be given a choice of companies to log into.**  
4. **Select the new company you want to join and click on its record**

**An alternative procedure to join a new company:**

1. **Navigate to *User Profile* \> *Company Management***  
2. **In the list of companies and active invitations, scroll to the invitation you need and click Join**

**After you have joined at least 2 companies, upon logging in, evalink talos allows you to select which company to log into.**

### **Switching between Different Companies**

**You can switch between several companies you are a member of using two options:**

* **When logging in, you are given a choice of all available companies you are a member of. You can log in to any of them**  
* **When logged in, go to *User Profile* \> *Company Management*, scroll to the company you want to switch to, and click \> next to its record**

### **Create a New Company**

**There is an option to create a company from scratch in your User Profile. For details, see section [First Steps in evalink talos \> Create a Company](https://documentation.evalink.io/talos/admin/introduction/first-steps-in-evalink-talos-for-admins#create-a-company).**

# **Manage Users**

**Only Administrator can manage users: invite users, change user roles, and delete users from the Company account.**

**These operations are performed on the *Company* \> *Users* page. The control elements on the page are described in section [View Users](https://documentation.evalink.io/talos/admin/work-with-users/view-users).**

## **Invite a User**

**See section [Work with Users \> Manage User Invites \> Invite a User](https://documentation.evalink.io/talos/admin/work-with-users/manage-user-invites#invite-a-user).**

## **Search for a User**

**To search for a particular user record:**

1. **Specify your search parameters in the filter bar**  
   **The figure below shows the available search parameters for users:**  
   **![][image8]**  
   **Available search parameters are:**  
   * **Name: type the user name or its part. The search field is dynamic, it returns all matching results**  
   * **Email: type the user e-mail or it part. The search field is dynamic, it returns all matching results**  
   * **Role: select one or several User Roles from the drop-down. The search returns all user records with the matching roles**  
   * **Workspace: select one or several Workspace from the drop-down. The search returns all users that have at least one of the selected Workspaces whitelisted for them**  
   * **Test Types: select one or several Test Types from the drop-down. The search returns all users that have at least one of the selected Test Types whitelisted for them**  
2. **Locate the necessary user record in the search results**  
3. ***(Optional)*** **Use the filter\_alt\_off icon to reset filters**

## **Edit a User**

**The only thing you can edit for a user record is the User Type. For details about User Types, see section [Users Overview \> User Types](https://documentation.evalink.io/talos/admin/work-with-users/users-overview#user-types).**

**To change the User Type:**

1. **Click the three dots icon more\_horiz for a particular user record**  
2. **From the context menu, select Edit User**  
   **![][image9]**  
3. **In the dialog that opens, select the desired User Type from the drop-down**

**Both preset and custom user types are available for selection.**

4. **Click Submit**

**info**

**You cannot change the User Type of the user under which you're currently logged in.**

## **Delete a User**

1. **Click the three dots icon more\_horiz for a particular user record**  
2. **From the context menu, select Remove User**  
3. **Specify a confirmation code and click Confirm Delete**

**info**  
**The user is deleted from the Company account, but their credentials (email and password) remain in evalink talos. The user can then join your or another Company account, if invited by Administrator, or create a new Company using these credentials.**

# **Work with User Role Permissions**

User role permissions define actions which are available in evalink talos for a particular user.

A set of user role permissions constitutes a User Role. An Administrator user has a set of all permissions, an Operator Minimal user has a set of limited permissions.

In evalink talos, when you assign a User Role to a user, you can select from 4 default user roles. Each of them has their own permission set. You can also create a custom user role and create a customer permission set for it.

Below is the reference list of every user role permission available in evalink talos. Permissions for core functionality have a comment which says not to disable them.

**warning**

Disabling core functionality permissions may lead to incorrect work of evalink talos and is not recommended.

| Permission | A | M | O | OM | Description | Comments |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- |
| Alarm:Create | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | Create new alarms in evalink talos UI. | Do not disable this setting for any user roles. |
| Alarm:Delete | \[x\] |  |  |  | Delete existing alarms. |  |
| Alarm:MassiveManagement | \[x\] | \[x\] |  |  | Enables bulk actions on multiple alarms simultaneously. |  |
| Alarm:Read | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | View alarm details. | Do not disable this setting for any roles. |
| Alarm:Update | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | Edit and modify alarm details. |  |
| Alarm:UpdateStatus | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | View alarm status (whether it's assigned, processed, dismissed, etc.) |  |
| AlarmDispatcher:Mutate | \[x\] | \[x\] |  |  | Update Global Alarm Dispatcher settings. |  |
| AlarmDispatcher:Read | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | View Alarm Dispatcher settings outside of Site/Company pages. |  |
| AlarmDistributionFilter:Mutate | \[x\] | \[x\] |  |  | In the Alarms page, you can view filters by pressing filter icon, but cannot change settings. |  |
| AlarmDistributionFilter:Read | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | In the Alarms page, the Filter icon becomes inactive if this is disabled. |  |
| AlarmSettings:Read | \[x\] | \[x\] |  |  | View global alarm settings in Company \> Settings. |  |
| AlarmSettings:Update | \[x\] |  |  |  | View or edit global alarm settings. |  |
| AlarmSignal:Acknowledge | \[x\] |  |  |  | Remove alarm signals from Alarm Dispatcher queue. |  |
| Analytics:Manage | \[x\] |  |  |  | View and manage analytics dashboards and reports. |  |
| Analytics:View | \[x\] | \[x\] |  |  | Access Analytics \> Insights from the dashboard. Can only be disabled if you disable 'Analytics:Manage. |  |
| ApiToken:Mutate | \[x\] |  |  |  | Edit API tokens for integrations. |  |
| ApiToken:Read | \[x\] |  |  |  | View API tokens for integrations. |  |
| Chat:Manage | \[x\] | \[x\] |  |  | Manage chat settings on the Company level and access all chats in your organization. |  |
| Company:ManageBilling | \[x\] |  |  |  | Access and manage company-level billing data. |  |
| Company:ManageGlobalEntities | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | Access to global-level contacts, schedules, global and managed workflows for operators outside of site or company pages. | Do not disable this setting for any roles. |
| Company:ManageSubscription | \[x\] |  |  |  | Manage your evalink talos subscription details. |  |
| Company:ManageUsers | \[x\] |  |  |  | Delete and add users, change user details. |  |
| Company:Read | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | Batch-service setting for company permissions. | Do not disable this setting for any roles. |
| Company:ReadSettings | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | Company-wide settings are available outside Company tab. | Don not disable this settings for any user roles. |
| Company:ReadUsageData | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] |  | Access to ccumulated data from various receivers for billing service. |  |
| Company:Update | \[x\] |  |  |  | Update general company information. |  |
| Company:UpdateSettings | \[x\] |  |  |  | Manage settings onCompany\>Settingspage. |  |
| Contact:Create | \[x\] | \[x\] |  |  | Add Contacts of any level. |  |
| Contact:Delete | \[x\] | \[x\] |  |  | Delete Contacts of any level. |  |
| Contact:ManageContactRoles | \[x\] | \[x\] |  |  | Edit Contact roles. |  |
| Contact:Read | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | View Contact details. | Do not disable this setting as it may cause errors when working with workflows. |
| Contact:Update | \[x\] | \[x\] |  |  | Edit Contact details. |  |
| ContactList:Create | \[x\] | \[x\] |  |  | Add Contact Lists of any level. |  |
| ContactList:Delete | \[x\] | \[x\] |  |  | Delete Contact Lists of any level. |  |
| ContactList:Read | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | View Contact List information. | Do not disable this setting as it may cause errors when working with workflows. |
| ContactList:Update | \[x\] | \[x\] |  |  | Edit Contact Lists. |  |
| CustomQuery:Mutate | \[x\] |  |  |  | Create and manage custom data sources for Advanced reporting (available only with evalink talos analytics). |  |
| CustomQuery:Read | \[x\] |  |  |  | View custom data sources for Advanced reporting (available only with evalink talos analytics). \\n. |  |
| CustomSla:Mutate | \[x\] | \[x\] |  |  | Define and view custom service level agreements. |  |
| CustomSla:Read | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] |  | View custom service level agreements. |  |
| Device:Create | \[x\] | \[x\] |  |  | Create and manage evalink talos sites. |  |
| Device:Delete | \[x\] | \[x\] |  |  | Delete evalink talos sites. |  |
| Device:ManageCustomAttributes | \[x\] | \[x\] |  |  | View and edit custom attributes for Sites. |  |
| Device:ManageStatusRoles | \[x\] | \[x\] |  |  | View and edit Site Status Roles. |  |
| Device:MutateAlarmSupervision | \[x\] | \[x\] |  |  | Edit Alarm Supervision settings for Sites. |  |
| Device:MutateArmRule | \[x\] | \[x\] |  |  | Edit Arm/Disarm rules for Sites. |  |
| Device:MutateCodeWord | \[x\] | \[x\] |  |  | Edit access code word for Sites. |  |
| Device:MutateCriteriaMapping | \[x\] | \[x\] |  |  | Edit Criteria Mapping for Sites. |  |
| Device:MutateFile | \[x\] | \[x\] |  |  | Edit files associated with Sites |  |
| Device:MutateNote | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | Add/Edit/Delete site notes from workflow view. |  |
| Device:MutatePartition | \[x\] | \[x\] |  |  | Edit partitions for Sites. |  |
| Device:MutateProblem | \[x\] | \[x\] |  |  | Access and change Site connection state. |  |
| Device:MutateShareLink | \[x\] | \[x\] |  |  | Create Site share links. |  |
| Device:MutateStatus | \[x\] | \[x\] |  |  | Manage Site Statuses. |  |
| Device:MutateZone | \[x\] | \[x\] |  |  | Edit zones for Sites. |  |
| Device:Read | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | Manage or view sites (devices) and their settings. |  |
| Device:ReadAlarmSupervision | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | View Site Alarm Supervision details outside of the Site page. |  |
| Device:ReadArmRule | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | View Site Arm/Disarm status outside of the Site page. |  |
| Device:ReadCodeWord | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | View Site code word outside of the Site age. |  |
| Device:ReadCriteriaMapping | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | View Site Criteria mappinng outside of the Site page, e.g., in workflows. |  |
| Device:ReadFile | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | View files associated with a sSite outside of the Site page, e.g. in workflows. |  |
| Device:ReadNote | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | Read Site notes outside of Site Page (from workflow view). |  |
| Device:ReadProblem | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | View Site's connection status outside of the Site Page, e.g., in Alarms Queue. |  |
| Device:ReadShareLink | \[x\] | \[x\] |  |  | View sharable links for a Site. |  |
| Device:ReadStatus | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | View Site Status outside of the Site page. |  |
| Device:ReadZone | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | View Zone information outside of the Site page, e.g. in Alarm details. |  |
| Device:Share | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] |  | Share link to the Site. |  |
| Device:Update | \[x\] | \[x\] |  |  | Edit Site details. |  |
| Device:UpdateTestMode | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] |  | Set the Test mode on or off for a Site. |  |
| DeviceGroup:Create | \[x\] | \[x\] |  |  | Create Site Groups. |  |
| DeviceGroup:Delete | \[x\] | \[x\] |  |  | Delete Site Groups. |  |
| DeviceGroup:MutateCodeWord | \[x\] | \[x\] |  |  | Edit Code word for Site Groups. |  |
| DeviceGroup:MutateCriteriaMapping | \[x\] | \[x\] |  |  | Configure Criteria Mappings for Site Groups. |  |
| DeviceGroup:MutateShareLink | \[x\] | \[x\] |  |  | Create Sharable Links for Site Groups. |  |
| DeviceGroup:Read | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | View Site Group information outside of Site Group and Site pages. |  |
| DeviceGroup:ReadCodeWord | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | View Site Group Code word outside of Site Group and Site pages. |  |
| DeviceGroup:ReadCriteriaMapping | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | View Criteria Mappings for the Site Group outside of Site Group o Site pages, e.g. in Alarms or Schedules. |  |
| DeviceGroup:ReadShareLink | \[x\] | \[x\] |  |  | View shareable link for the Site Group. |  |
| DeviceGroup:Update | \[x\] | \[x\] |  |  | Edit Site Group details. |  |
| DeviceTag:Mutate | \[x\] | \[x\] |  |  | Edit tags for a Site. |  |
| DeviceTag:Read | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | View Site tag outside of the Site page. |  |
| Email:Send | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | Permission to send emails from evalink talos profile.Do not disable for operators who work with workflows eith 'send email' steps |  |
| Evalink:ReadStatus | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | Read system status of the Evalink talos platform. | Do Not disable this setting for any User Roles so that operators know at all times if they are online. |
| EventLog:Create | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | Add an event log entry. | Do not disable this setting for any user roles, as it can break alarm logging. |
| EventLog:Delete | \[x\] |  |  |  | A setting available for automated workflows: the PDF event log report is not emailed to any recipients and is erased from the Event Logs page after the workflow execution is completed\\n. |  |
| EventLog:Read | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | Access to Events page. |  |
| EventLog:Update | \[x\] |  |  |  | Update event log information, currently not in use. |  |
| File:Create | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | Add files to Alarms and Workflows. | Do not disable if working with alarms that deal with files, such as video feeds and image verifications. |
| File:Delete | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | Delete files from Alarms and Workflows. | Do not disable if working with alarms that deal with files, such as video feeds and image verifications. |
| File:Read | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | View files associated with Alarms and Workflows . | Do not disable if alarms with workflows that deal with files, such as video feeds and image verifications. |
| File:Update | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | Update a file on site, currently not in use. |  |
| FollowUp:Create | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | Create or follow-up events. |  |
| FollowUp:Delete | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | Delete follow-up events. |  |
| FollowUp:Update | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | Edit or manage follow-up events. |  |
| Integration:Mutate | \[x\] |  |  |  | Configure integrations on a global level. |  |
| Integration:Read | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | View global integration details outside of Site pages. |  |
| IntegrationResources:Mutate | \[x\] | \[x\] |  |  | Configure integrations in a Site level. |  |
| IntegrationResources:Read | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | View site-specific integration details outside of Site pages. |  |
| Job:ManageCustomAttributes | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | Edit custom Attributes for Jobs. |  |
| Job:Mutate | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | Edit a Job. |  |
| Job:Read | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | View Job information. | Do not disable for any user roles. |
| JobType:Mutate | \[x\] | \[x\] |  |  | Edit Job types. |  |
| JobType:Read | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | View Job Type information. | Do not disable for any user roles. |
| Log:Read | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] |  | View logs. |  |
| LogNotificationRule:Mutate | \[x\] | \[x\] |  |  | Edit rules for log notifications. |  |
| LogNotificationRule:Read | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] |  | View rules for log notifications. |  |
| Message:Read | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | Manage settings of a particular chat. |  |
| Message:Send | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | Send messages to users or to chat. |  |
| MessageConverter:Read | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | Allow custom alarm parcing from virtual receivers. |  |
| MonitoringStation:Create | \[x\] |  |  |  | Create Monitoring Stations. |  |
| MonitoringStation:Delete | \[x\] |  |  |  | Delete Monitoring Stations. |  |
| MonitoringStation:Read | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] |  | View Monitoring Station details. |  |
| MonitoringStation:Update | \[x\] |  |  |  | Edit Monitoring Station details. |  |
| OperatorActivity:Read | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | View online/offline and availability status of operators. | Do not disable for any user roles, as this could break the Alarm view. |
| OperatorActivity:Update | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | Change your operator status. |  |
| Organization:ManageCollaboration | \[x\] |  |  |  | Obsolete |  |
| Organization:Read | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | Obsolete |  |
| Organization:ReadCollaboration | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | Obsolete |  |
| Organization:ReadProfile | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | Obsolete |  |
| Organization:UpdateProfile | \[x\] |  |  |  | Obsolete |  |
| PdfSettings:Manage | \[x\] | \[x\] |  |  | Manage PDF Report settings on Company Level. Disabling it doesn't remove relevant controls, only disables them. |  |
| PdfSettings:Mutate | \[x\] | \[x\] |  |  | Mail PDF controls for sites. Disabling doesn't remove Mail PDF controls, only disables them. |  |
| PdfSettings:Read | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | Enables 'Export PDF' button on the UI | Do not disable this setting, as it may disable all pdf-related controls. |
| PushNotificationFilter:Manage | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] |  | Manage push notification filters for mobile app. |  |
| ReportSettings:Read | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | View CSV report settings. |  |
| ReportSettings:Update | \[x\] |  |  |  | Enables Next Generation Reports Toggle. |  |
| Schedule:AddEntry | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | Add entries to schedules. |  |
| Schedule:Create | \[x\] | \[x\] |  |  | Create schedules. |  |
| Schedule:Delete | \[x\] | \[x\] |  |  | Delete schedules. |  |
| Schedule:ManageScheduleRoles | \[x\] | \[x\] |  |  | Edit Schedule Roles. |  |
| Schedule:ManageSpecialDays | \[x\] | \[x\] |  |  | Edit Special days for schedules. |  |
| Schedule:Read | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | View schedules outside of Site or Site group pages. |  |
| Schedule:Update | \[x\] | \[x\] |  |  | Edit schedules. |  |
| Sequence:Mutate | \[x\] | \[x\] |  |  | Set sequence value for the Sequence header in event message . |  |
| Sequence:Read | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] |  | View sequence value. |  |
| SiteTemplate:CreateSite | \[x\] | \[x\] |  |  | Create site from a template. |  |
| SiteTemplate:ManageSiteTemplates | \[x\] | \[x\] |  |  | Manage site templates. |  |
| SiteTemplate:Read | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] |  | View site templates. |  |
| TestModeType:Mutate | \[x\] |  |  |  | Modify test mode types. |  |
| TextBlock:Mutate | \[x\] | \[x\] |  |  | Edit Predefined Text Blocks in Company Settings Reports |  |
| TextBlock:Read | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | View text blocks details outside of Site pages. |  |
| UI:DismissAlarms | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] |  | Dismiss alarms from evalink talos UI (Alarm dashboard or Alarm view). |  |
| UI:ReOpenWorkflows | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] |  | Re-open closed (not parked) workflows from the event log, from **more actions** button |  |
| UI:ViewAlarms | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | Makes the navigation bar in the **Alarms** tab available on dashboard | Do not disable for any roles. |
| UI:ViewCompany | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] |  | Navigation bar Company tab: **Manage Connection**, **Manage Integrations** buttons (on dashboard) |  |
| UI:ViewDevices | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] |  | Access Sites page and individual Sites in evalink talos UI,, navigation bar for Sites/Site Groups |  |
| UI:ViewEventLogs | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | Access Event Logs page in evalink talos UI. Navigation bar for **Event Log** tab, **View Event Logs** button on dashboard | Do not disable for any roles. |
| UI:ViewEvents | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | Access Events page in evalink talos UI. | Do not disable for any roles. |
| UI:ViewIntegrations | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] |  | Adds global integrations like EagleEye or Milestone to the global navigation bar. |  |
| UI:ViewStatuses | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | Add Analytics to the global navigation bar. |  |
| User:Read | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | Shows company users recent activity in the global navigation bar. |  |
| UserAlarmDistribution:Mutate | \[x\] | \[x\] |  |  | Assign and reassign alarms. |  |
| UserAlarmDistribution:Read | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | View alarm assignments. |  |
| UserRole:Mutate | \[x\] |  |  |  | Edit User roles and permission sets. |  |
| UserRole:Read | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | View user roles outside of settings pages and user lists (e.g. for active operators in Alarms page). |  |
| VoipCall:DownloadRecording | \[x\] | \[x\] |  |  | Download VoIP call recordings. |  |
| VoipCall:Read | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | View VoIP call details. |  |
| VoipCall:Update | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | Make VoIP calls within workflows. | Do not disable if you want to provide an operator with the ability to use Twilio integration |
| Workflow:Cancel | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | Cancel workflow processing. | Do not disable any workflow permissions for any user roles. Workflow permissions are core functionality of evalink talos. |
| Workflow:CancelExecution | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | You can cancel a workflow assigned to you from the Workflow view of Alarms page. |  |
| Workflow:Create | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | Creat a new workflow. |  |
| Workflow:Delete | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | Delete a workdlow. |  |
| Workflow:Execute | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | Run automated steps in workflow. Disabling this step will still allow to start manual workflows. |  |
| Workflow:Read | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | View workflow details. |  |
| Workflow:Reassign | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | Reassign workflows. |  |
| Workflow:SendPdf | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | Send PDF reports from worflows. |  |
| Workflow:Update | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | Update workflow processing status. |  |
| WorkflowSettings:Read | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | View workflow settings in Alarm processing window. |  |
| WorkflowSettings:Update | \[x\] |  |  |  | Configure workflow settings on a global level. |  |
| WorkflowTemplate:Create | \[x\] | \[x\] |  |  | Create workflow templates. |  |
| WorkflowTemplate:Delete | \[x\] | \[x\] |  |  | Delete workflow templates. |  |
| WorkflowTemplate:Read | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | \[x\] | View workflow templates. |  |
| WorkflowTemplate:Update | \[x\] | \[x\] |  |  | Update workflow templates. |  |
